Terms & Conditions +

1. Confidentiality

Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality regarding all business information belonging to the service provider. The recipient shall not disclose any confidential information to any third party without prior written consent. This obligation remains in effect during the term of this Agreement and for five (5) years thereafter.

2. Cancellations

Cancellation fees apply based on the notice period provided:

  • 30% fee — if notice is given 7 days or more before the scheduled start date.
  • 50% fee — if notice is given less than 7 days before the start date.
  • 100% fee — if notice is given less than 24 hours before the start date.

Cancellations made at least one month in advance, confirmed in writing, will not incur any fees if reasonable efforts are made to avoid cancellation.

3. Payment Terms

Invoices may be issued following the first round of revisions or upon delivery of footage. The total quoted cost, including expenses and any agreed price adjustments, must be paid within one (1) calendar month of the final day of production.

Payments must be made via bank transfer only. Late payments will incur a 5% charge, compounded every 14 days, beginning after the one-month payment window.

4. Revisions and Approvals

Two (2) rounds of minor revisions are included in the quoted fee. Additional revisions or significant creative changes will be charged separately at the service provider's standard hourly rate.

Clients must provide feedback or approval within two (2) working days of receiving revised materials to keep the project on schedule. Delays in client responses may affect delivery timelines without penalty to the service provider.

5. Intellectual Property and Usage Rights

The service provider retains full copyright and ownership of all creative materials until full payment has been received. Upon payment, the client receives non-exclusive, unlimited rights to use the final deliverables for their intended purpose.

The service provider retains the right to use completed work for promotional and portfolio use unless expressly prohibited in writing.

6. Expenses and Additional Costs

Out-of-pocket expenses (including travel, accommodation, materials, or other project-specific costs) will be invoiced in addition to the quoted amount, subject to prior agreement.

7. Force Majeure

Neither party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by circumstances beyond reasonable control, including natural disasters, strikes, illness, equipment failure, or government actions. Each party will notify the other promptly upon becoming aware of such circumstances.

8. Termination

Either party may terminate this Agreement in writing if the other materially breaches its obligations and fails to remedy such breach within 10 business days of notice. Termination does not absolve the client of payment obligations for work completed before termination.

9. Limitation of Liability

The service provider's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total amount paid by the client for the services rendered. The provider shall not be liable for indirect, consequential, or incidental damages.

10. Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

11. Agreement

By signing this document, both parties confirm they have read, understood, and agree to the terms and conditions outlined in this quotation.
